findings.description: `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/work-packages.yaml` correctly restricts `src/api/app.py` to a single writer (`wp-audit`) and denies `wp-api`, but this contradicts `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/design.md` (Section D7) which dictates `wp-api` must write to `app.py` to register its routers. Consequently, `wp-api` has no mechanism to mount its new routers because it is denied from `app.py` and lacks access to any other router registry. Furthermore, `wp-audit` (priority 2) cannot mount routers that `wp-api` (priority 3) has not yet created.
findings.resolution: Grant `wp-api` write access to a central router module (e.g., `src/api/routes/__init__.py`) to mount its routers, and have `wp-audit` mount that single module in `app.py`. Update `design.md` D7 to reflect this delegation.

findings.description: `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/proposal.md` (Section 4) requires tagging existing destructive endpoints (`DELETE /topics/{slug}`, `POST /kb/purge`, `POST /manage/switch-embeddings`) with `@audited`, but `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/tasks.md` (Phase 1) omits tasks for this. Additionally, `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/work-packages.yaml` (`wp-api` definition) lacks `write_allow` access to `src/api/routes/manage_routes.py` to tag the embeddings endpoint.
findings.resolution: Add a task in Phase 1 (`tasks.md`) to apply the `@audited` decorator to the existing destructive endpoints, and add `src/api/routes/manage_routes.py` to `wp-api`'s `write_allow` list in `work-packages.yaml`.

findings.description: `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/work-packages.yaml` assigns `wp-ops` a priority of 4, contradicting `openspec/changes/cloud-db-source-of-truth/design.md` (Section D7) which assigns it priority 2 to run in parallel with `wp-audit`.
